summary:
[summary here]

Summary: Recurrent fungal and bacterial infections involving *Aspergillus*, *Pseudomonas*, and *S. aureus* are characteristic of Chronic Granulomatous Disease (CGD), which results from defects in phagocyte-mediated reactive oxygen species generation via the NADPH oxidase system. Failure of the nitroblue tetrozolium test confirms absence of respiratory burst, indicating dysfunction of the NADPH oxidase complex responsible for initiating microbial kill mechanisms through superoxide production.

Summary: Recurrent fungal and bacterial infections involving *Aspergillus*, *Pseudomonas*, and *S. aureus* are characteristic of Chronic Granulomatous Disease (CGD), which results from defects in phagocyte-mediated reactive oxygen species generation via the NADPH oxidase system. Failure of the nitroblue tetrozolium test confirms absence of respiratory burst, indicating dysfunction of the NADPH oxidase complex responsible for initiating microbial kill mechanisms through superoxide production.. Answer choice: D
